Hidden Fragility in Financial Markets¶

A Human-Body Network Diagnostic Framework with Nonlinear Early-Warning¶

In [1]:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import networkx as nx
import statsmodels.api as sm
import pyarrow.parquet as pq
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.dates as mdates
import warnings
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

DATA_DIR = r"C:\Users\David\Desktop\Folders\For David"
OUT_DIR  = r"C:\Users\David\Desktop\Folders\VIk_Project\Financial Markets and Institutions"

SECTOR_MAP = {
    10: 'Energy', 15: 'Materials', 20: 'Industrials',
    25: 'ConsumerDisc', 30: 'ConsumerStap', 35: 'HealthCare',
    40: 'Financials', 45: 'InfoTech', 50: 'CommServices', 55: 'Utilities'
}
SAMPLE_START = '2000-01-01'
SAMPLE_END   = '2024-12-31'
WINDOW       = 60
FORWARD_DAYS = 20
DD_THRESHOLD = -0.08

1. Load CRSP Data and Build Sector Returns¶

In [2]:
print('Loading CRSP data...')
f    = pq.ParquetFile(f"{DATA_DIR}/dailySP500_fundQ.parquet")
cols = ['permno', 'date', 'prc', 'ret', 'shrout', 'gsector']
chunks = []
for i in range(f.num_row_groups):
    batch = f.read_row_group(i, columns=cols).to_pandas()
    batch = batch[
        (batch['date'] >= SAMPLE_START) & (batch['date'] <= SAMPLE_END) &
        batch['gsector'].notna() & batch['ret'].notna() &
        batch['prc'].notna() & batch['shrout'].notna() & (batch['shrout'] > 0)
    ].copy()
    batch['gsector'] = batch['gsector'].astype(int)
    batch = batch[batch['gsector'].isin(SECTOR_MAP)]
    chunks.append(batch)
    print(f"  Row group {i+1}/{f.num_row_groups} done")

crsp = pd.concat(chunks, ignore_index=True)
crsp['ret_dec'] = crsp['ret'] / 100
crsp['mktcap']  = crsp['prc'].abs() * crsp['shrout']
print(f"Loaded {len(crsp):,} rows")

Loaded 2,888,454 rows
In [3]:
print('Building value-weighted sector returns...')
def vw_ret(g):
    w = g['mktcap'] / g['mktcap'].sum()
    return (w * g['ret_dec']).sum()

sec = (
    crsp.groupby(['date', 'gsector']).apply(vw_ret)
    .reset_index(name='ret')
    .pivot(index='date', columns='gsector', values='ret')
)
sec.columns = [SECTOR_MAP[c] for c in sec.columns]
sec = sec.sort_index().dropna(how='all')
print(f"Sector matrix: {sec.shape}")
sec.tail(3).round(5)

Building value-weighted sector returns...
Sector matrix: (6289, 10)

2. Load SPY and VIX¶

In [4]:
spy = (
    pd.read_csv(f"{DATA_DIR}/spy_daily_return.csv", parse_dates=['date'])
    .set_index('date').rename(columns={'sprtrn': 'spy_ret'})[['spy_ret']]
)
spy = spy[(spy.index >= SAMPLE_START) & (spy.index <= SAMPLE_END)]

vix = (
    pd.read_csv(f"{DATA_DIR}/VIX_History.csv", parse_dates=['DATE'])
    .set_index('DATE').rename(columns={'CLOSE': 'vix'})[['vix']]
)
vix = vix[(vix.index >= SAMPLE_START) & (vix.index <= SAMPLE_END)]

print('SPY:', spy.shape, '| VIX:', vix.shape)

SPY: (6289, 1) | VIX: (6309, 1)

3. Build Rolling Network and Extract Biomarkers¶

Four biomarkers per 60-day rolling window:

  • Density — proportion of sector pairs with |correlation| > 0.5
  • Clustering — how tightly sectors form interconnected groups
  • Max centrality — dominance of the most connected sector
  • Avg weight — average pairwise correlation strength
In [5]:
def compute_biomarkers(window):
    corr = window.corr().abs()
    np.fill_diagonal(corr.values, 0)
    n     = corr.shape[0]
    G     = nx.from_pandas_adjacency(corr)
    upper = corr.values[np.triu_indices(n, k=1)]
    density    = (upper > 0.5).mean()
    avg_weight = upper.mean()
    clustering = nx.average_clustering(G, weight='weight')
    try:
        ec = nx.eigenvector_centrality_numpy(G, weight='weight')
        max_centrality = max(ec.values())
    except Exception:
        max_centrality = np.nan
    return {'density': density, 'clustering': clustering,
            'max_centrality': max_centrality, 'avg_weight': avg_weight}

print(f"Computing {WINDOW}-day rolling biomarkers...")
dates, biomarkers = sec.index, []
for i in range(WINDOW, len(dates)):
    w     = sec.iloc[i - WINDOW:i]
    valid = w.dropna(axis=1, thresh=WINDOW // 2).columns
    if len(valid) < 8:
        continue
    bm = compute_biomarkers(w[valid])
    bm['date'] = dates[i]
    biomarkers.append(bm)

bio = pd.DataFrame(biomarkers).set_index('date')
print(f"Biomarkers computed for {len(bio):,} dates")
bio.tail(3).round(4)

Computing 60-day rolling biomarkers...
Biomarkers computed for 6,229 dates
Out[5]:
density clustering max_centrality avg_weight
date

2024-12-30 0.2222 0.3599 0.4235 0.3549
2024-12-31 0.2444 0.3739 0.4182 0.3675

4. Build Dependent Variable and Master Dataset¶

In [6]:
spy['fwd_ret_20'] = (
    (1 + spy['spy_ret']).rolling(FORWARD_DAYS).apply(np.prod, raw=True)
    .shift(-FORWARD_DAYS) - 1
)
spy['event']        = (spy['fwd_ret_20'] <= DD_THRESHOLD).astype(int)
spy['realized_vol'] = spy['spy_ret'].rolling(WINDOW).std() * np.sqrt(252)
print(f"Event rate: {spy['event'].mean():.2%} | Stress dates: {spy['event'].sum()}")

Event rate: 4.18% | Stress dates: 263
In [7]:
bio_cols = ['density', 'clustering', 'max_centrality', 'avg_weight']
master   = bio.join(spy[['spy_ret', 'realized_vol', 'event']], how='left').join(vix, how='left').dropna()

master['fragility']      = StandardScaler().fit_transform(master[bio_cols]).mean(axis=1)
master['high_fragility'] = (master['fragility'] > master['fragility'].quantile(0.80)).astype(int)
master['fragility_chg_5']  = master['fragility'].diff(5)
master['fragility_chg_20'] = master['fragility'].diff(20)
master = master.dropna()
y   = master['event']
q80 = master['fragility'].quantile(0.80)

master.to_csv(f"{OUT_DIR}/master_dataset.csv")
print(f"Master dataset: {master.shape}")
master.describe().round(4)

Master dataset: (6209, 12)

5. Biomarker Time Series (H1 — Hidden Illness)¶

In [8]:
crisis_shades = [
    ('2007-10-01', '2009-03-31', 'GFC'),
    ('2020-02-01', '2020-04-30', 'COVID'),
    ('2022-01-01', '2022-10-31', '2022 Bear'),
]
fig, axes = plt.subplots(4, 1, figsize=(14, 12), sharex=True)
labels = ['Density', 'Clustering', 'Max Centrality', 'Avg Edge Weight']
for ax, col, lbl in zip(axes, bio_cols, labels):
    ax.plot(master.index, master[col], linewidth=0.8, color='steelblue')
    for s, e, name in crisis_shades:
        ax.axvspan(pd.Timestamp(s), pd.Timestamp(e), alpha=0.15, color='red', label=name)
    ax.set_ylabel(lbl, fontsize=9)
    ax.grid(alpha=0.3)
axes[0].legend(loc='upper left', fontsize=8)
axes[-1].xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter('%Y'))
fig.suptitle('Network Biomarkers Over Time (60-day rolling window)', fontsize=12)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(f"{OUT_DIR}/biomarkers_timeseries.png", dpi=150)
plt.show()

6. Logit Models (H2, H3)¶

In [9]:
fit_kw = dict(cov_type='HAC', cov_kwds={'maxlags': 20}, disp=False)

X_vix    = sm.add_constant(master[['spy_ret', 'vix', 'realized_vol']])
X_base   = sm.add_constant(master[bio_cols + ['spy_ret', 'vix', 'realized_vol']])
X_thresh = sm.add_constant(master[['fragility', 'high_fragility', 'spy_ret', 'vix', 'realized_vol']])
X_speed  = sm.add_constant(master[['fragility', 'fragility_chg_5', 'fragility_chg_20',
                                    'high_fragility', 'spy_ret', 'vix', 'realized_vol']])

logit_vix    = sm.Logit(y, X_vix).fit(**fit_kw)
logit_base   = sm.Logit(y, X_base).fit(**fit_kw)
logit_thresh = sm.Logit(y, X_thresh).fit(**fit_kw)
logit_speed  = sm.Logit(y, X_speed).fit(**fit_kw)

print('All models fitted.')

All models fitted.

7. Threshold Effect Chart (H2 — Tipping Point)¶

In [13]:
frag_range  = np.linspace(master['fragility'].min(), master['fragility'].max(), 200)
mean_others = master[['spy_ret', 'vix', 'realized_vol']].mean()
pred_df = pd.DataFrame({
    'const': 1, 'fragility': frag_range,
    'high_fragility': (frag_range > q80).astype(int),
    'spy_ret': mean_others['spy_ret'],
    'vix': mean_others['vix'],
    'realized_vol': mean_others['realized_vol'],
})
plt.figure(figsize=(9, 4))
plt.plot(frag_range, logit_thresh.predict(pred_df), color='steelblue', linewidth=2)
plt.axvline(q80, color='red', linestyle='--', label=f'80th pct = {q80:.2f}')
plt.xlabel('Composite Fragility Score')
plt.ylabel('P(20-day drawdown >= 8%)')
plt.title('Nonlinear Effect of Market Fragility on Drawdown Probability')
plt.legend()
plt.grid(alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(f"{OUT_DIR}/threshold_effect.png", dpi=150)
plt.show()

8. Model Comparison Table (H3 — Beats VIX)¶

In [14]:
comparison = pd.DataFrame({
    'Model':          ['VIX-only', 'Baseline', 'Threshold', 'Speed-enhanced'],
    'Pseudo-R2':      [logit_vix.prsquared, logit_base.prsquared,
                       logit_thresh.prsquared, logit_speed.prsquared],
    'AUC':            [roc_auc_score(y, logit_vix.predict(X_vix)),
                       roc_auc_score(y, logit_base.predict(X_base)),
                       roc_auc_score(y, logit_thresh.predict(X_thresh)),
                       roc_auc_score(y, logit_speed.predict(X_speed))],
    'Log-likelihood': [logit_vix.llf, logit_base.llf, logit_thresh.llf, logit_speed.llf],
    'AIC':            [logit_vix.aic, logit_base.aic, logit_thresh.aic, logit_speed.aic],
})
print(comparison.round(4).to_string(index=False))
comparison.to_csv(f"{OUT_DIR}/model_comparison.csv", index=False)

         Model  Pseudo-R2    AUC  Log-likelihood       AIC
      VIX-only     0.0579 0.7368      -1025.8595 2059.7190

Speed-enhanced     0.0846 0.7403       -996.6837 2009.3673

9. Recovery Analysis (H4 — Slower Recovery)¶

In [15]:
shock_dates_clean = []
last_shock = pd.Timestamp('1900-01-01')
for d in master[master['spy_ret'] < -0.02].index:
    if (d - last_shock).days > 30:
        shock_dates_clean.append(d)
        last_shock = d
print(f"Independent shock events: {len(shock_dates_clean)}")

records = []
for shock in shock_dates_clean:
    pre = master.loc[:shock, 'fragility'].iloc[-6:-1]
    if len(pre) < 3:
        continue
    pre_vol  = master.loc[:shock, 'realized_vol'].iloc[-21:-1].mean()
    post     = master.loc[shock:].iloc[1:]
    recovered = post[post['realized_vol'] <= pre_vol * 1.1]
    rec_days  = (recovered.index[0] - shock).days if len(recovered) > 0 else np.nan
    records.append({
        'shock_date': shock, 'recovery_days': rec_days,
        'pre_fragility': pre.mean(),
        'spy_ret_shock': master.loc[shock, 'spy_ret'],
        'vix_shock': master.loc[shock, 'vix'],
    })

rec_df = pd.DataFrame(records).dropna()
rec_df.to_csv(f"{OUT_DIR}/recovery_analysis.csv", index=False)
print(f"Usable shock events: {len(rec_df)}")
rec_df.describe().round(2)

Independent shock events: 92
Usable shock events: 92

In [16]:
X_rec = sm.add_constant(rec_df[['pre_fragility', 'spy_ret_shock', 'vix_shock']])
ols_rec = sm.OLS(rec_df['recovery_days'], X_rec).fit(cov_type='HC3')
print(ols_rec.summary())

Notes:
[1] Standard Errors are heteroscedasticity robust (HC3)
[2] The condition number is large, 5.23e+03. This might indicate that there are
strong multicollinearity or other numerical problems.
In [17]:
plt.figure(figsize=(7, 4))
plt.scatter(rec_df['pre_fragility'], rec_df['recovery_days'],
            alpha=0.5, edgecolors='k', linewidths=0.4, s=40)
m, b = np.polyfit(rec_df['pre_fragility'], rec_df['recovery_days'], 1)
xs = np.linspace(rec_df['pre_fragility'].min(), rec_df['pre_fragility'].max(), 100)
plt.plot(xs, m * xs + b, color='red', linewidth=1.5, label='OLS fit')
plt.xlabel('Pre-shock Composite Fragility')
plt.ylabel('Recovery Days')
plt.title('H4: Higher Fragility -> Slower Recovery')
plt.legend()
plt.grid(alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(f"{OUT_DIR}/recovery_scatter.png", dpi=150)
plt.show()

10. Markov-Switching Model (Regime Detection)¶

In [18]:
ms_model = sm.tsa.MarkovRegression(master['fragility'], k_regimes=2, trend='c', switching_variance=True)
ms_res   = ms_model.fit(disp=False)
print(ms_res.summary())

                        Markov Switching Model Results

                 coef    std err          z      P>|z|      [0.025      0.975]
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
p[0->0]        0.9942      0.001    753.727      0.000       0.992       0.997
p[1->0]        0.0065      0.002      4.290      0.000       0.004       0.009
==============================================================================

Warnings:
[1] Covariance matrix calculated using numerical (complex-step) differentiation.
In [19]:
regime1_prob = ms_res.smoothed_marginal_probabilities[1]
fig, (ax1, ax2) = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(14, 6), sharex=True)
ax1.plot(master.index, master['fragility'], linewidth=0.8, color='steelblue')
ax1.set_ylabel('Composite Fragility')
ax1.grid(alpha=0.3)
ax2.fill_between(master.index, regime1_prob, alpha=0.6, color='tomato')
ax2.set_ylabel('P(Fragile Regime)')
ax2.set_ylim(0, 1)
ax2.grid(alpha=0.3)
for s, e, name in crisis_shades:
    ax2.axvspan(pd.Timestamp(s), pd.Timestamp(e), alpha=0.15, color='navy', label=name)
ax2.legend(loc='upper left', fontsize=8)
ax2.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter('%Y'))
fig.suptitle('Markov-Switching: Fragile vs. Stable Regime Probabilities', fontsize=12)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(f"{OUT_DIR}/markov_switching.png", dpi=150)
plt.show()

11. Out-of-Sample Test — Train on 2000-2010, Predict 2011-2024¶

In [20]:
# Split into train (2000-2010) and test (2011-2024)
train = master[master.index <= '2010-12-31']
test  = master[master.index  > '2010-12-31']

print(f"Train: {train.shape} | {train.index.min().date()} to {train.index.max().date()}")
print(f"Test:  {test.shape}  | {test.index.min().date()}  to {test.index.max().date()}")
print(f"Train event rate: {train['event'].mean():.2%}")
print(f"Test  event rate: {test['event'].mean():.2%}")

Train: (2687, 12) | 2000-04-27 to 2010-12-31
Test:  (3522, 12)  | 2011-01-03  to 2024-12-31
Train event rate: 6.29%
Test  event rate: 2.67%
In [21]:
# Train all four models on 2000-2010 only
y_train = train['event']
fit_kw  = dict(cov_type='HAC', cov_kwds={'maxlags': 20}, disp=False)

X_vix_tr    = sm.add_constant(train[['spy_ret', 'vix', 'realized_vol']])
X_base_tr   = sm.add_constant(train[bio_cols + ['spy_ret', 'vix', 'realized_vol']])
X_thresh_tr = sm.add_constant(train[['fragility', 'high_fragility', 'spy_ret', 'vix', 'realized_vol']])
X_speed_tr  = sm.add_constant(train[['fragility', 'fragility_chg_5', 'fragility_chg_20',
                                      'high_fragility', 'spy_ret', 'vix', 'realized_vol']])

m_vix    = sm.Logit(y_train, X_vix_tr).fit(**fit_kw)
m_base   = sm.Logit(y_train, X_base_tr).fit(**fit_kw)
m_thresh = sm.Logit(y_train, X_thresh_tr).fit(**fit_kw)
m_speed  = sm.Logit(y_train, X_speed_tr).fit(**fit_kw)

print('Models trained on 2000-2010.')

Models trained on 2000-2010.
In [22]:
# Predict on 2011-2024 (data the model has never seen)
y_test = test['event']

X_vix_te    = sm.add_constant(test[['spy_ret', 'vix', 'realized_vol']])
X_base_te   = sm.add_constant(test[bio_cols + ['spy_ret', 'vix', 'realized_vol']])
X_thresh_te = sm.add_constant(test[['fragility', 'high_fragility', 'spy_ret', 'vix', 'realized_vol']])
X_speed_te  = sm.add_constant(test[['fragility', 'fragility_chg_5', 'fragility_chg_20',
                                     'high_fragility', 'spy_ret', 'vix', 'realized_vol']])

# Use training coefficients to predict on test data
p_vix_oos    = m_vix.predict(X_vix_te)
p_base_oos   = m_base.predict(X_base_te)
p_thresh_oos = m_thresh.predict(X_thresh_te)
p_speed_oos  = m_speed.predict(X_speed_te)

oos = pd.DataFrame({
    'Model':     ['VIX-only', 'Baseline', 'Threshold', 'Speed-enhanced'],
    'OOS AUC':   [
        roc_auc_score(y_test, p_vix_oos),
        roc_auc_score(y_test, p_base_oos),
        roc_auc_score(y_test, p_thresh_oos),
        roc_auc_score(y_test, p_speed_oos),
    ],
})

print("=== OUT-OF-SAMPLE AUC (trained 2000-2010, tested 2011-2024) ===")
print(oos.round(4).to_string(index=False))
oos.to_csv(f"{OUT_DIR}/oos_comparison.csv", index=False)

=== OUT-OF-SAMPLE AUC (trained 2000-2010, tested 2011-2024) ===
         Model  OOS AUC
      VIX-only   0.6449
      Baseline   0.6693
     Threshold   0.6030
Speed-enhanced   0.6528
In [23]:
# Plot predicted crash probability vs actual events (2011-2024)
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 4))
ax.plot(test.index, p_base_oos, linewidth=0.8, color='steelblue', label='Baseline predicted P(crash)')
ax.fill_between(test.index, y_test, alpha=0.3, color='red', label='Actual crash events')
for s, e, name in crisis_shades:
    if pd.Timestamp(s) > pd.Timestamp('2011-01-01'):
        ax.axvspan(pd.Timestamp(s), pd.Timestamp(e), alpha=0.1, color='navy', label=name)
ax.set_ylabel('P(20-day drawdown >= 8%)')
ax.set_title('Out-of-Sample Predictions: Baseline Model Trained on 2000-2010')
ax.legend(loc='upper left', fontsize=8)
ax.grid(alpha=0.3)
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter('%Y'))
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(f"{OUT_DIR}/oos_predictions.png", dpi=150)
plt.show()
In [24]:
# Zoom into crisis windows to check whether predictions LEAD or LAG crashes
windows = [
    ('2019-09-01', '2020-06-30', 'COVID-19 Crash (Feb–Mar 2020)'),
    ('2021-07-01', '2022-12-31', '2022 Bear Market'),
]

fig, axes = plt.subplots(len(windows), 1, figsize=(14, 7))

for ax, (start, end, title) in zip(axes, windows):
    mask = (test.index >= start) & (test.index <= end)
    t    = test.index[mask]
    p    = p_speed_oos[mask]   # use speed-enhanced model — most sensitive
    ev   = y_test[mask]

    ax.plot(t, p, color='steelblue', linewidth=1.2, label='Speed-enhanced P(crash)')
    ax.fill_between(t, ev.values * p.max(), alpha=0.35, color='red', label='Actual crash days')
    ax.set_title(title, fontsize=10)
    ax.set_ylabel('P(crash)')
    ax.legend(fontsize=8)
    ax.grid(alpha=0.3)
    ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter('%b %Y'))
    ax.xaxis.set_major_locator(mdates.MonthLocator(interval=2))
    plt.setp(ax.xaxis.get_majorticklabels(), rotation=30)

fig.suptitle('OOS Predictions — Zoomed Crisis Windows\n(Does the model spike BEFORE or DURING crashes?)', fontsize=11)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(f"{OUT_DIR}/oos_zoom.png", dpi=150)
plt.show()
